title: Expel Illegal Chinese Police Act of 2025
short_title: Expel Illegal Chinese Police Act of 2025
plain_summary: This bill requires the President to punish Chinese police departments and organizations that try to operate in the United States by freezing their money and property in America. It also bans employees of these Chinese law enforcement groups and their family members from entering the US and cancels any visas they already have. The bill targets Chinese organizations that secretly watch or threaten people living in America. Additionally, it stops US government agencies from working with these banned Chinese groups on investigations unless the US government starts the investigation or the President decides it is very important for keeping Americans safe.
last_action_text: Read twice and referred to the Committee on Foreign Relations.

sections.title: Section 1 Short Title
sections.text: This law can be called the Expel Illegal Chinese Police Act of twenty twenty five.

sections.title: Section 2 Sanctions with respect to police departments in the People's Republic of China
sections.text: Starting when this law takes effect, the President must punish certain people and organizations from China. The President must freeze the money and property of any foreign person who is a police department or law enforcement agency in China, including those in Xinjiang Uyghur region and Fujian province. This also applies to senior leaders of these departments, anyone helping to set up Chinese police operations in the United States, anyone working under Chinese Public Security Bureaus or law enforcement agencies, and anyone working with the United Front Work Department to secretly watch or threaten people living in America. The President must also ban certain people from entering the United States. This includes employees of Chinese police departments or the United Front Work Department, their immediate family members like spouses, parents, siblings, and adult children, and anyone directly involved in setting up Chinese police or United Front Work Department operations in America. For property blocking, the President must use all powers from the International Emergency Economic Powers Act to freeze and stop all business deals involving property owned by the targeted Chinese organizations if that property is in the United States or controlled by Americans. For immigration bans, targeted people cannot get visas to enter the United States, cannot be admitted into the country, and cannot get any benefits under immigration law. Any visas these people already have will be canceled immediately. The President can temporarily lift these punishments for up to thirty days at a time if the President tells Congress at least fifteen days ahead of time that doing so is vital to America's national security. The President can send this explanation to Congress in secret form if needed. The President can use all powers from sections two hundred three and two hundred five of the International Emergency Economic Powers Act to enforce this law. Anyone who breaks this law or tries to break it will face the same penalties as those in section two hundred six of that same act. The President must tell all federal agencies not to help with any investigation started by another country into the targeted Chinese people unless the President decides that helping is vital to the health, safety, and well being of American citizens. In this section, admitted and alien have the same meanings as in section one hundred one of the Immigration and Nationality Act. A foreign person means someone who is not an American person. An immediate family member means a spouse, parent, sibling, or adult child. A person means an individual or organization, including a government organization. A United States person means an American citizen, a foreign person legally allowed to live permanently in America, an organization created under American laws including foreign branches of such organizations, or any person in the United States.
